title: Phase 1E: Tensor::as_strided + stride-tricks via ferray-stride-tricks
description:
Add as_strided and friends as a Tensor method, wrapping ferray-stride-tricks. Closes the indexing gap flagged in docs/audit/01-ferrotorch-core.md (was: 'as_strided' missing).

torch.Tensor methods to add:
- as_strided(size, stride, storage_offset) -> Tensor — view with arbitrary stride pattern
- as_strided_copy — same but materialized
- as_strided_scatter — write into an as_strided view

Scope:
- Add ferray-stride-tricks = workspace dep in ferrotorch-core/Cargo.toml.
- Add methods on Tensor<T> in ferrotorch-core/src/methods.rs (or a new stride_tricks.rs) that delegate to ferray-stride-tricks while preserving Tensor metadata (device, autograd, grad fn).
- Wire autograd: as_strided's backward is as_strided_scatter; copy/scatter form a triple. See torch source for the gradient formula.
- Tests: verify that stride manipulation works for (a) shape -> reshape view, (b) stride -> strided view, (c) backward over an as_strided view sums correctly into the larger tensor.

GPU discipline:
- as_strided is a METADATA operation. It does NOT touch storage bytes — it constructs a new TensorStorage view with adjusted strides/offset. This works identically on CPU and GPU storage; no per-device dispatch needed.
- BUT: for GPU tensors, the resulting view must reuse the same GpuBufferHandle (refcount the underlying CudaBuffer). Verify this with a test that creates a large CUDA tensor, takes an as_strided view, drops the original, and confirms the view still works (Drop semantics).
- as_strided_copy DOES touch bytes. Implement via the existing memcpy path: for CUDA tensors, dispatch to a GPU kernel that copies indexed source -> contiguous dest. Add to GpuBackend trait if not present. /rust-gpu-discipline forbidden pattern: copying GPU bytes through host RAM.
- as_strided_scatter is the reverse: read from a contiguous source, write into the strided view. Same GPU dispatch story.

Watch out for unsafe semantics: as_strided lets users construct overlapping views, which torch documents as 'undefined behavior on write'. Mirror that warning in the docstring.
